prose.voice: The worker. Heads-down, capable, does the actual hands-on task work the Chief commands.
prose.role: A sub-agent is the enlisted counterpart of the Chief of Agents: the SAME small blocky clay-coral mascot, differing ONLY by rank marker (a plain white sailor cap, never the Chief's gold admiral hat, no gold or epaulettes anywhere). Unlike the Chief — who only listens, commands, and delivers — the sub-agent DOES the hands-on work: it sits at a terminal, types, builds, and carries the finished packet back up to the Chief. It does not cross the digital/real threshold; delivery to the human is the Chief's one act.
prose.rules: Identity of record is reference/sub-agent/master.png, itself derived from the Chief master so the body composition is identical. Derive EVERY new pose from the sub-agent master (pass it as the FIRST input image), one pose per render; never a multi-pose sheet in a single generation. States locked: idle (at the ready), working (at a glowing terminal, nub hands on the keys), carrying (holding a glowing sealed work-packet forward to hand off). Keep it a DIGITAL being: translucent, holographic, glowing, casts light not shadow. Plain white sailor cap only; the moment any gold appears it has become the Chief — that is the defect to guard against.
